skill.name: create-git-worktree
skill.description: Create an isolated Git worktree from a branch, update the base branch, and copy local environment settings for setup.
skill.summary: Automates Git worktree creation for isolated branch work.
skill.author: AKABANAKK

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution and parent-directory filesystem writes are mostly expected for a Git worktree helper. The confirmed concern is copying .env into a new worktree, which can duplicate local secrets and should be disclosed before publication.

medium_findings.title: Environment File Copied Into Worktree
medium_findings.description: The script checks for .env and copies it into the new worktree. This is local behavior, but it can duplicate secrets into another directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly copies .env into the worktree, and the skill description states that .env setup is automatic. I found no network exfiltration, so the risk is secret duplication rather than theft.

medium_findings.title: External Git Commands Modify Local Repository State
medium_findings.description: The script runs git checkout, git pull, and git worktree commands. This is expected, but it can change the current checkout and contact the configured Git remote.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and central to the skill purpose. Arguments are quoted, so I did not find shell command injection evidence.

low_findings.title: Parent Directory Worktree Path Is Intentional
low_findings.description: The script creates worktrees in a sibling directory using a parent path. This writes outside the repository, but it matches the worktree setup goal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent path is hardcoded from the repository root and quoted. The branch slash replacement reduces traversal risk, though the script still writes outside the repo.

low_findings.title: Static Prompt And Entropy Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The markdown code fence only shows how to run the script. I found no prompt injection text, weak cryptographic operation, or obfuscated payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain front matter and a normal shell command example. No suspicious instruction override or hidden content is present.

low_findings.title: Command Substitution Findings Are Benign
low_findings.description: The command substitutions derive names and Git metadata. They do not execute user-controlled shell text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The branch name is quoted before transformation, and the other substitutions call fixed Git or basename commands. I found no eval, backticks, or unquoted shell expansion.

dangerous_patterns.title: Copies Local Secret File
dangerous_patterns.description: The script copies .env into the worktree when the file exists. Users should confirm that duplicated secrets are acceptable.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The copy operation is explicit and uses a common secret file name. The risk is local exposure, not confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runs Repository-Changing Git Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The script checks out the base branch, pulls updates, and creates a worktree. These commands can change working state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Git commands are directly present and intentional for this skill. No malicious remote or hidden command chain was found.

content.value_statement: Branch setup can waste time when each task needs a clean workspace. This skill creates a Git worktree and prepares local environment files.
actual_capabilities: Creates a Git worktree for a specified branch.
Uses the current branch as the default base branch.
Accepts a custom base branch with the --from option.
Stores worktrees in a sibling worktrees directory.
Reuses an existing worktree when the path already exists.
Copies a local .env file into the new worktree.
limitations: Requires Git and a valid repository checkout.
Runs a shell script on the local machine.
Can change the current branch before creating the worktree.
Does not install npm dependencies despite the description mentioning setup.

use_cases.title: Start Feature Work
use_cases.description: Create a separate worktree for a new feature branch without disturbing the main checkout.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Open an existing branch in its own directory for testing and review.

use_cases.title: Prepare Parallel Tasks
use_cases.description: Set up separate branch directories for several active tasks in one repository.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Worktree
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Git worktree for branch feature/login-flow.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one feature branch.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Base Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Git worktree for branch feature/payment-retry from main.
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a new branch from a specific base.

prompt_templates.title: Reuse If Present
prompt_templates.prompt: Create or reuse a worktree for bugfix/session-timeout and show the next directory to open.
prompt_templates.scenario: Continue work when the worktree may already exist.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Review Workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a worktree for review/pr-128 from develop, then summarize any repository state changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review workflow with awareness of local changes.

output_examples.output: The assistant creates a sibling worktree directory, checks out the branch, and reports the path to open next.

output_examples.input: Create a worktree for hotfix/token-refresh from main.
output_examples.output: The assistant updates the base branch, creates the hotfix worktree, and copies the local environment file when present.

output_examples.input: Open the existing worktree for feature/reporting.
output_examples.output: The assistant detects the existing worktree and returns the directory path without recreating it.

best_practices: Check for uncommitted work before running branch-changing commands.
Review .env contents before copying secrets into another directory.
Use clear branch names that match team naming rules.
anti_patterns: Do not run this outside a Git repository.
Do not use it when .env must stay in one protected location.
Do not assume it installs dependencies without verifying the script.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a Git worktree for a branch in a sibling worktrees directory.

faq.question: Can I choose the base branch?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --from option to set the base branch.

faq.question: Does it copy environment variables?
faq.answer: Yes. It copies .env when that file exists in the repository root.

faq.question: Does it install dependencies?
faq.answer: No evidence found in the script that it installs npm or other dependencies.

faq.question: Will it overwrite an existing worktree?
faq.answer: No. It detects an existing worktree path and reuses it.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. It can copy local secrets and run git pull against the configured remote.
